summary: Tested iteration-3 NEW features: (a) rebrand SourceHQ→AskWinn across Navbar/Footer/Landing/index.html, (b) localStorage key rename sourcehq_desired_role→askwinn_desired_role, (c) POST /api/rfqs/{id}/quotes optional contact_number, (d) POST /api/rfqs/{id}/accept close-RFQ flow with 4 negative cases, (e) RFQDetail UI: quote-contact field + Accept/close button + WINNER/NOT SELECTED badges + agent form hidden on close. 30/30 backend pytests green (23 regression + 7 new). All frontend assertions green.

minor.issue: No idempotency — second call on same already-closed RFQ returns 400 (correct), but also: if the quote_id passed does not belong to that rfq_id, the endpoint's find filter ({quote_id, rfq_id}) correctly returns 404. However, a buyer could pass quote_id belonging to ANOTHER of their own RFQs and receive 404 with a confusing 'Quote not found' message — consider 400 'Quote does not belong to this RFQ' for clarity.

Auto-message writes 'Congratulations — you won the RFQ …' but does NOT insert the mirror message into agent's list-threads view reliably: list_threads groups by other_user_id which works, but there is no notification/unread flag, so agent must manually poll /messages/thread/{buyer_id}. Consider an unread_count field on messages collection.

issues: public/index.html has correct '<title>AskWinn — Win your next manufacturing partner</title>' but the runtime document.title renders as 'Emergent | Fullstack App' — Emergent preview/dev wrapper is overriding it. Not an app-level bug; flagged for awareness. In production build this should pick up index.html title.

action_items: (Optional polish) Return 400 with clearer message when accept quote_id does not belong to rfq_id.
(Optional polish) Ensure document.title is set to AskWinn at runtime (useEffect in root App or React Helmet) so Emergent dev wrapper doesn't show 'Emergent | Fullstack App'.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py accept_quote: thread_key(buyer_id, agent_user_id) is deterministic — good. But the auto-message is inserted even if the thread already has messages, producing a duplicate 'Congratulations' on re-accept (currently blocked by 400 on closed RFQ, so safe).

server.py line 25: ADMIN_EMAIL is still 'admin@sourcehq.test' — post-rebrand this hardcoded test email still references old brand. Consider 'admin@askwinn.test' or env-driven ADMIN_EMAIL. (Note: test_credentials.md and tests still expect admin@sourcehq.test, so keep backward compat if you rename.)
server.py get('service'): returns {'service':'SourceHQ'} on GET /api/ — brand string leak; update to 'AskWinn' for consistency.
RFQDetail.jsx accepting flow: uses window.confirm() — for nicer UX, swap to a shadcn AlertDialog but functionally correct.
RFQDetail.jsx: after accept, code does load() to refresh. Good. But Agent-side still sees 'CLOSED' tag with no contextual copy (e.g., 'This RFQ has been awarded to X'). Nice-to-have for transparency.

Quote model defaults: contact_number='' and status='pending'. Older quotes inserted before iter-3 won't have status field; the GET /rfqs/{id} returns them without status — fine for frontend because it falls back to rfq.winner_quote_id===q.quote_id for the isWinner check. Verified in test.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py (+7 new tests for contact_number + accept flow; total 30)
test_credentials: Sessions seeded via mongosh/pymongo per /app/auth_testing.md. Admin email: admin@sourcehq.test (unchanged — see review comment).
seed_data_creation: Pytest fixtures seed TEST_ buyer/agent/admin + accept_setup (1 RFQ + 2 agents + 2 quotes) and clean up in teardown. UI test seeded 1 buyer + 2 agents + 1 RFQ + 1 quote; cleaned up post-run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 3 is fully green. Backend pytest file at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py now has 30 tests — use as regression suite. Frontend data-testids on RFQDetail: quote-form, quote-price, quote-lead, quote-contact, quote-message, quote-submit, quote-{quote_id}, quote-chat-{quote_id}. Accept button has no dedicated testid — selector was `button:has-text('Accept')` — consider adding data-testid={`accept-${quote_id}`} in next iter. confirm() was stubbed via page.on('dialog', lambda d: asyncio.create_task(d.accept())). Brand leak: server.py returns 'SourceHQ' on GET /api/ and ADMIN_EMAIL is admin@sourcehq.test — minor consistency fixes. public/index.html <title> is correct but runtime title overridden by Emergent preview wrapper.
rca of the issue: N/A — no blocking bug. Only cosmetic brand leaks in backend (/api/ root, ADMIN_EMAIL) and document.title override by preview wrapper.
